core_question: Does gain neuromodulation driven by stimulus ambiguity cause task-relevant perceptual switches?
key_assumptions: pupil diameter indirectly indexes phasic neuromodulatory tone
stimulus ambiguity drives neuromodulatory tone and neural gain
RNN dynamics are an analogue for cortical perceptual categorisation
manipulations: ambiguous figures perceptual task with subjective switches
RNN gain allowed to scale dynamically with classification uncertainty
low-dimensional projection of RNN and brain dynamics
key_findings: perceptual switches coincide with peaks in pupil diameter
higher RNN gain accelerates perceptual switches by destabilising dynamics under uncertainty
fMRI switches align with peaks in low-dimensional brain state velocity
fMRI switches align with a flattened egocentric energy landscape
interpretations: task-relevant perceptual switches are mediated by uncertainty-driven gain neuromodulation
macroscale dynamical signatures (velocity, landscape) index neuromodulatory reset
